substrate_constraint: Most S9A members act mainly on oligopeptides because the N-terminal beta-propeller restricts access to the catalytic site
Serine carboxypeptidases remove residues from a free protein or peptide C terminus rather than defining an internal cleavage motif
Leishmanolysin-family metallopeptidases are endopeptidases; family assignment alone does not establish the physiological substrate
Most M14 enzymes hydrolyse single C-terminal residues and recognize a free C-terminal carboxyl group
